original_hypothesis.title: RandomizerNXT updateAdminsContract Missing isAdminContract Validation Enables Privilege Escalation via Fake Admin Contract
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: RandomizerNXT.updateAdminsContract sets the adminsContract without validating isAdminContract() == true, unlike all other contracts in the system (RandomizerVRF.updateAdminContract, RandomizerRNG.updateAdminContract, NextGenCore.updateAdminContract, MinterContract.updateAdminContract all include this check). A function admin for updateAdminsContract can set a malicious contract that returns true for all admin checks, effectively granting any address unrestricted access to all RandomizerNXT admin functions including updateCoreContract (enabling hash manipulation).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an asymmetric validation defect - all other contracts validate the admin contract via isAdminContract(), but RandomizerNXT does not. A function admin (lower privilege than global admin) could escalate to control the randomizer by setting a permissive fake admin contract.

proof_of_signal.pattern: updateAdminsContract(address _admin) sets adminsContract = INextGenAdmins(_admin) without require(INextGenAdmins(_admin).isAdminContract() == true) - compare with RandomizerVRF.sol:86-88

preconditions: Attacker has function admin permission for updateAdminsContract selector on RandomizerNXT
Attacker deploys a malicious contract that returns true for all admin queries

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Missing validation: no isAdminContract() check in RandomizerNXT vs present in all other contracts
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Set fake admin → gain control of updateCoreContract → point to fake gencore → manipulate token hashes
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker could control hash generation to produce desirable/rare traits, selling high-value NFTs

economic_questions: Can a function admin for this specific selector escalate to control the randomizer?
What is the downstream impact of controlling the randomizer?
Is the missing check an intentional omission or a bug?
validation_requirements: Compare updateAdminsContract across all contracts for consistency
Deploy fake admin contract and verify function admin can set it
Test if fake admin enables unauthorized access to other functions

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: ~0.01 ETH to deploy fake admin contract
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Value of manipulated NFT traits on secondary market
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Single transaction for escalation; ongoing hash manipulation

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All minters of collections using this randomizer
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Value of all tokens with manipulated hashes

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Compare validation patterns across all update functions

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
